questions.question: What happens when you set width on an inline element?
questions.explanation: Inline elements ignore width and height. Use inline-block or block if you need to set dimensions.

questions.question: What's the difference between display: none and visibility: hidden?
options: display: none removes from flow; visibility: hidden preserves space
visibility: none removes from flow; display preserves space
display: none works on all elements; visibility only on block
questions.explanation: display: none removes the element from document flow entirely. visibility: hidden hides it but it still occupies space.

questions.question: Which display value allows elements to sit inline while respecting width/height?
questions.explanation: inline-block flows inline like text but respects all box model properties including width and height.

questions.question: What is the default display value for a <div> element?
questions.explanation: The default display for <div> is block — it takes up the full available width and starts on a new line.

questions.question: What is the default display value for a <span> element?
questions.explanation: The default display for <span> is inline — it flows within text and doesn't start a new line.

questions.question: What does display: flex enable?
options: Flexible box layout with powerful alignment and distribution capabilities
questions.explanation: display: flex creates a flex container, enabling flexible layout with alignment, ordering, and space distribution.

questions.question: What does display: flow-root do?
options: Creates a new block formatting context, preventing margin collapse and containing floats
Makes an element a flex container and root
questions.explanation: display: flow-root creates a new block formatting context without side effects, commonly used to contain floats.

questions.question: What happens when display: contents is applied?
options: The element's box is removed, but its children render normally
The element shows its content only
The element becomes a flex container
questions.explanation: display: contents removes the element's box from the layout while its children are rendered as if the wrapper doesn't exist.

questions.question: Why might display: none be problematic for accessibility?
options: Content with display: none is hidden from screen readers
questions.explanation: display: none removes content from the accessibility tree, hiding it from screen readers.

questions.question: What is the default display value for elements?
questions.explanation: Images default to display: inline but are 'replaced elements', which means they behave differently from regular inline elements.

questions.question: What display values activate CSS Grid layout?
questions.explanation: display: grid creates a block-level grid container. display: inline-grid creates an inline-level grid container.

questions.question: How do block elements behave differently from inline elements?
options: Block elements start on new lines and fill available width; inline elements flow in text
Block elements can't have padding; inline elements can
Block elements are always square; inline are rectangular
questions.explanation: Block elements start on a new line and fill available width. Inline elements flow within text and shrink to content width.

questions.question: What happens when you change a <span> to display: block?
options: It starts on a new line and fills the available width
questions.explanation: Changing an inline element to display: block makes it start on a new line and fill available horizontal space.

questions.question: What does display: table do to a non-table element?
options: Makes the element behave like a <table> element for layout purposes
questions.explanation: display: table (and display: table-row, table-cell) makes elements mimic table layout behavior.

questions.question: Can display values be animated?
options: No, display cannot be animated with CSS transitions
Yes, display transitions work in all browsers
Only display: none to block works
questions.explanation: display cannot be animated with CSS transitions. Use opacity or visibility for animated show/hide effects.

questions.question: What is the purpose of display: inline-flex?
options: Creates an inline block with flex children
questions.explanation: display: inline-flex creates a flex container that flows inline with surrounding content, rather than on a new block line.

questions.question: What display value should you use for a horizontal navigation menu?
questions.explanation: display: flex on the <ul> creates a horizontal layout with alignment and spacing control for navigation items.

questions.question: What is the difference between inline-block and inline?
options: inline-block respects width/height and top/bottom margins/padding; inline doesn't
inline-block is always square; inline is rectangular
questions.explanation: inline-block flows inline but respects all box model properties (width, height, top/bottom margin/padding).

questions.question: What does setting display: none on a parent do to its children?
options: Children are also hidden since the parent's box is removed
questions.explanation: When a parent has display: none, it and all its children are removed from the layout and accessibility tree.

questions.question: What is the default display value for <li> elements?
questions.explanation: <li> elements default to display: list-item, which is similar to block but also generates a marker box.
